_comment: Runtime overlay registry consumed by 'atmosphere new <name> --runtime <X>'. Each overlay declares the Maven dependencies (and optional repository) that must be appended to the scaffolded sample's pom.xml so the named AgentRuntime is on the classpath. Atmosphere's AgentRuntime SPI picks the highest-priority runtime present, so adding the overlay deps to a transparent sample like spring-boot-ai-chat is enough to swap providers.

builtin.description: Built-in OpenAI-compatible runtime (no extra deps; default)

koog.description: JetBrains Koog (Kotlin — works from Java via JVM interop)

agentscope.description: AgentScope Java (Tongyi Lab) — Reactor-based ReActAgent with Flux<Event> streaming
agentscope.note: Requires a user-supplied ReActAgent @Bean — AgentScope's Model surface (DashScope, OpenAI-compatible) needs provider-specific construction that can't be assembled from generic llm.* properties.

spring-ai-alibaba.description: Spring AI Alibaba (Alibaba Cloud AI) — Spring AI extension with multi-agent / graph orchestration; buffered streaming
spring-ai-alibaba.note: Targets Spring Boot 3.5 — build with -Pspring-boot3 (Spring AI Alibaba 1.1.2.0 transitively pulls Spring AI 1.1.2 which references Spring Boot 3.x autoconfigure classes that don't exist in SB4). Streaming is buffered: ReactAgent.call() is sync, so the full reply ships as one chunk through Atmosphere transport (no incremental token deltas from the LLM). Provide your own ReactAgent @Bean to add tools / sub-agents / custom graph compile config; the autoconfig builds a default agent on top of any Spring AI ChatModel bean.

embabel.description: Embabel GOAP agent platform (Spring Boot 3.5 only)
embabel.note: Embabel targets Spring Boot 3.5 — build with -Pspring-boot3 (Atmosphere's parent profile pins SB 3.5 + atmosphere-spring-boot3-starter).
